paragraph: Sovereign Realty Capital completed the acquisition of Thornfield Exchange, a 487,000-square-foot Class A office complex in the Westbridge district of Calderton, for $214.5 million, or approximately $440 per square foot, according to sources familiar with the transaction. The seller, Meridian Crown Partners, had held the asset since 2017, when it acquired the two-tower campus for $161 million. Thornfield Exchange was 91 percent occupied at closing, anchored by a regional headquarters lease with Farwest Financial Group covering 112,000 square feet through March 2034. The deal was financed with a $138 million senior loan from Duncaster Institutional Lenders at a floating rate of SOFR plus 185 basis points, maturing in five years with two one-year extension options. The going-in capitalization rate was reported at 5.4 percent. Sovereign Realty Capital chief investment officer Harrison Velt indicated the firm intends to invest an additional $18 million in lobby modernization, HVAC upgrades, and tenant amenity buildouts over the next 24 months. Eastbridge Realty Advisors represented the seller, while Sovereign Realty Capital was represented internally. The transaction closed on April 22, 2026, and marks Sovereign Realty Capital's fourth acquisition in the Calderton metropolitan statistical area since 2021.

paragraph: Halverson Property Trust announced a $392 million recapitalization of its Northgate Logistics Portfolio, a collection of eight industrial warehouse facilities totaling 3.1 million square feet spread across the Lakemont industrial corridor in the city of Veldran. The transaction involved the sale of a 49 percent joint venture interest to Penrose Infrastructure Partners, a Luxembourg-domiciled open-end fund, at a blended valuation of $126 per square foot. The portfolio is 98.3 percent leased to fourteen tenants, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 7.2 years. Major occupants include Crestline Distribution Services, Orion Consumer Fulfillment, and Taber Freight Systems. The recap proceeds will be used to retire $175 million in secured debt maturing in August 2026 and to fund a 680,000-square-foot build-to-suit expansion at the Northgate West sub-campus, scheduled for delivery in the fourth quarter of 2027. The capitalization rate implied by the transaction was 4.85 percent. Veldran industrial vacancy stood at 3.9 percent at the time of signing. Cormorant Capital Markets arranged the joint venture equity and facilitated debt placement with Aldwick Life Insurance at a fixed rate of 5.62 percent over ten years.

paragraph: Pelham Square Hotel Group, a privately held hospitality investor, closed on the $98.7 million purchase of the Ardmore Grand, a 312-key full-service hotel situated at the intersection of Colonnade Boulevard and Fifth Meridian Street in downtown Keswick. The seller, Westmoor Hospitality Ventures, had originally developed the property in 2009 at a cost of $74 million. The Ardmore Grand carries a Courtyard-equivalent flag from a fictional lodging brand, Pinnacle Select Hotels, under a franchise agreement expiring in 2031. The hotel reported trailing twelve-month revenue per available room of $147, a gross operating profit margin of 34.2 percent, and an average daily rate of $189 for fiscal year 2025. Pelham Square financed the acquisition with a $62 million floating-rate bridge loan from Silverton Mortgage Capital, priced at SOFR plus 220 basis points, and approximately $36.7 million in equity. The buyer intends to invest $14 million in a phased renovation of all guest rooms, the ballroom, and the rooftop bar by mid-2027, targeting an ADR of $215 and RevPAR of $168 upon stabilization. Greyfield Hotel Advisors brokered the transaction on behalf of the seller. The deal represents the largest hotel trade in the Keswick CBD since the $104 million sale of the Ridgemoor Suites in 2023.

paragraph: Aurelia Life Sciences Partners acquired the Oakthorn Research Campus, a 215,000-square-foot life sciences facility in Portsmith's Bayside Innovation District, for $187 million, equating to $870 per square foot. The campus consists of three interconnected laboratory and office buildings, all constructed between 2018 and 2021, with specialized wet-lab infrastructure including 14-foot clear heights, 15 watts per square foot of power capacity, and enhanced HVAC systems meeting ASHRAE 90.1 standards. The seller, Inland Coast Opportunity Partners, acquired the land in 2015 and delivered the first building in 2018. At closing, the campus was 94 percent leased to six tenants, led by Varex Genomics, which occupies 88,000 square feet under a lease running through December 2030. The going-in cap rate was 4.6 percent. Financing consisted of a $110 million ten-year fixed-rate mortgage from Chandler National Life at 5.85 percent and a $32 million mezzanine tranche provided by Quartus Credit Partners at an all-in rate of 8.4 percent. Aurelia intends to develop an adjacent 90,000-square-foot shell building on a 1.8-acre land parcel included in the sale, with construction anticipated to begin in the first quarter of 2027. Stonebridge Property Advisors represented the seller.

paragraph: Caldwell Retail Opportunity Trust, a non-traded REIT focused on necessity-based retail, completed the $45.3 million purchase of Fernwood Village Center, a 198,000-square-foot grocery-anchored shopping center located in the Fernwood submarket of Orrenton. The center is anchored by a 54,000-square-foot Garfield Foods supermarket operating under a lease expiring in 2036, with eight-percent rent bumps every five years. Additional tenants include a national pharmacy, a regional fitness operator occupying 22,000 square feet, and eighteen inline merchants. Total center occupancy at closing stood at 96.2 percent, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 5.8 years. The transaction was financed with a $28.5 million ten-year fixed-rate loan from Portside Savings Bank at 5.45 percent interest only for the first five years. The going-in capitalization rate was 6.15 percent. Caldwell Retail Opportunity Trust's portfolio now encompasses 34 properties totaling 4.7 million square feet in 11 states. The transaction was sourced off-market through a long-standing relationship with the seller, Hillborn Retail Ventures. Delray Property Brokers acted as transaction advisor to the seller, and the deal closed on March 15, 2026.

paragraph: Glenmore Data Facilities, a developer and operator of hyperscale data centers, secured a $275 million construction loan from a syndicate led by Rivergate Commercial Bank and Northcliff Institutional Lending to fund development of the Halcyon Data Hub, a 240,000-square-foot, 60-megawatt campus situated on a 34-acre site in the Southfield Technology Corridor of Maplewood. paragraph: Stratford Residential REIT completed the $321 million acquisition of the Elmwood Crossings multifamily portfolio, comprising four garden-style apartment communities totaling 1,842 units located in the northern suburbs of Halcrest. The portfolio was assembled by Dunmore Capital Group between 2014 and 2019 at a total cost of approximately $198 million. The four communities range in age from seven to twelve years, with a combined average unit size of 882 square feet and an average monthly rent of $1,385 per unit at closing. Portfolio-wide occupancy was 95.1 percent at the time of sale. The transaction implies a per-unit price of $174,268 and a going-in cap rate of 5.05 percent. Stratford Residential REIT assumed $147 million in existing agency debt, comprising two Fandon Mae and one Fredmont Mac loans, with a blended interest rate of 3.89 percent and a remaining weighted average term of 6.4 years. Additional financing consisted of a $52 million mezzanine loan from Crestview Alternative Credit at 7.8 percent. Stratford indicated plans to invest $28 million across the portfolio in kitchen and bathroom unit upgrades, with targeted rent premiums of $150 to $220 per month upon renovation. NHM Realty Advisors represented the seller.

paragraph: Vantage Industrial Partners closed a $68.4 million refinancing of the Broadmoor Logistics Center, a 612,000-square-foot single-tenant distribution facility located at 4400 Meridian Commerce Parkway in the East Portway industrial submarket of Dunford. Loan-to-value at origination is 58 percent, based on a third-party appraisal of $118 million. The refinancing replaces a $44.5 million floating-rate construction loan from 2021. Proceeds include $12 million in cash-out, which Vantage intends to deploy toward land acquisition for a planned 400,000-square-foot speculative industrial facility in an adjacent submarket. The Broadmoor Logistics Center features 36-foot clear heights, 78 dock-high doors, four drive-in ramps, and a 185-foot truck court. Current net operating income is approximately $6.3 million annually. Dunford's East Portway submarket vacancy rate was 4.1 percent at the time of refinancing, according to Cornhill Property Research.

paragraph: Prestige Office Partners executed a 99-year ground lease on the 1.7-acre Harwick Parcel in downtown Thornbury, providing the foundation for a planned 620,000-square-foot mixed-use tower combining Class A office space, ground-floor retail, and a 180-key boutique hotel. The land is owned by the Thornbury Municipal Pension Authority, which will receive an initial annual ground rent of $4.2 million, subject to CPI-linked adjustments every five years capped at 2.5 percent per annum. Prestige Office Partners secured $445 million in construction financing led by Cortland Investment Bank and Kessler Mortgage Trust, with the loan bearing interest at SOFR plus 210 basis points. Total project cost is estimated at $690 million, with a projected stabilized yield on cost of 6.8 percent. The office component spans 420,000 square feet, the hotel 148,000 square feet, and retail accounts for 52,000 square feet across the ground and second floors. Prestige has secured a pre-lease commitment from Whitmore Consulting Partners covering 95,000 square feet of office space for 12 years. Groundbreaking is targeted for September 2026, with delivery scheduled for the third quarter of 2029. Westpoint Realty Finance advised Prestige on the ground-lease structure.

paragraph: Kestrel Realty Income Trust, a publicly traded net-lease REIT with a market capitalization of approximately $3.8 billion, announced the acquisition of a 24-property single-tenant portfolio for $312.6 million, reflecting a blended going-in cap rate of 5.7 percent. The acquisition increases Kestrel's total portfolio to 1,247 properties in 39 states, with aggregate annualized base rent of $218 million. Kestrel's board approved a $0.042 per share increase to the monthly dividend coincident with the announcement.

paragraph: Ironwood Capital Group completed the sale of the Harborview Plaza office tower, a 38-story, 775,000-square-foot Class A high-rise in the Central Business District of Portcliffe, for $589 million, equating to $760 per square foot. Ironwood's internal rate of return on the investment was reported at approximately 14.7 percent. Greystone Property Advisors and Corum Realty Services jointly represented the seller, while Arcadia was advised by Halverson Realty Capital.

paragraph: Dunmore Realty Corp completed a $134 million construction-to-permanent loan financing of Clearwater Commons, a 389-unit Class A multifamily community under development at 9200 Clearwater Drive in the Riverside submarket of Fenwick. paragraph: Aldgate Industrial Partners announced the 100 percent leasing of Aldgate Industrial Park, Phase II, a speculative 1.24-million-square-foot industrial development completed in November 2025 in the Southbrook Commerce Zone of Radford. Aldgate Industrial Partners is now advancing Phase III, a 1.8-million-square-foot expansion on an adjacent 95-acre parcel, with site work expected to begin in August 2026. Redfern Industrial Brokerage represented tenants in both final transactions.

paragraph: Portsmouth Value-Add Fund IV, managed by Cornerstone Real Estate Advisors, acquired the Eastgate Business Park, a 14-building suburban office campus totaling 820,000 square feet in the Eastgate submarket of Coverton, for $71.8 million, or $87.56 per square foot. The seller, a regional family office, had owned the campus since 1998. At acquisition, the park was 67 percent occupied, with in-place net operating income of approximately $4.1 million, implying an acquisition cap rate of 5.7 percent on current income. Cornerstone's business plan calls for converting the four lowest-occupied buildings, totaling 248,000 square feet, to flexible life sciences lab suites and creative office space, an effort budgeted at $38 million over 24 months. The remaining 572,000-square-foot conventional office component is slated for leasing at market rents of $22 to $28 per square foot, full service. Financing was provided by Belfort Commercial Lending in the form of a $48 million floating-rate bridge loan at SOFR plus 275 basis points with a 36-month initial term. Cornerstone anticipates a stabilized value of $155 million upon completion of the conversion and occupancy normalization. Meridian Office Advisors handled the transaction on behalf of the seller.

paragraph: Westmoor Mortgage Trust, a commercial mortgage REIT, originated a $178 million senior mortgage loan secured by the Mariner's Wharf mixed-use development in the Harborside neighborhood of Saltport. The collateral consists of a 410-unit luxury multifamily tower and 32,000 square feet of ground-floor retail space, all delivered in January 2026. The loan carries a floating interest rate of SOFR plus 195 basis points with a two-year initial term and three one-year extension options, subject to performance hurdles. The multifamily component was 78 percent leased at loan origination, with average effective rents of $2,340 per month across studio, one-, and two-bedroom configurations. The retail component is 65 percent leased, with four signed leases to food-and-beverage and fitness tenants. As-stabilized appraised value was reported at $297 million, implying a loan-to-value ratio of 59.9 percent at origination. The borrower, Harborside Realty Development, is a local developer that broke ground on the project in March 2023. Westmoor Mortgage Trust's chief credit officer noted that the Saltport apartment market recorded year-over-year effective rent growth of 4.2 percent in 2025, supporting the underwriting thesis. Galloway Capital Markets arranged the financing.

paragraph: Pemberton Square Realty Trust, a publicly traded diversified REIT, announced the disposition of the Crestview Tech Center, a four-building, 565,000-square-foot suburban office campus in the Crestview submarket of Alderton, for $101.5 million, or $179.65 per square foot. The buyer, a private equity fund advised by Thornton Value Investors, acquired the campus with plans for a conversion to last-mile logistics and light industrial use. Pemberton Square Realty Trust acquired the Crestview Tech Center in 2014 for $187 million and had absorbed declining occupancy since the pandemic, with in-place occupancy at closing of 58 percent. The sale price implies a current cap rate of 4.1 percent on in-place income and a significant write-down from book value. Pemberton Square Realty Trust will record an impairment charge of approximately $61 million in the second quarter of 2026. Proceeds will be redeployed into industrial and data center acquisitions consistent with the company's stated portfolio repositioning strategy. The transaction closed on May 7, 2026. Corum Realty Advisors and Alderton Property Services jointly arranged the sale. The disposition reduces Pemberton Square's office allocation from 28 percent to 22 percent of total portfolio net asset value.

paragraph: Cascade Realty Partners closed the acquisition of the Whitmore Industrial Gateway, a newly constructed 2.05-million-square-foot industrial park located on a 148-acre site in the exurban Eastbrook submarket of Vernondale, for $231 million, or approximately $112.68 per square foot. The seller, Ridgeline Development Group, delivered the project in two phases between Q3 2024 and Q1 2026. The park is fully leased to three tenants — Northfield Consumer Logistics, Vantage Global Distribution, and Elmore Building Products — under triple-net leases with remaining terms of 10, 12, and 8 years, respectively. Blended base rent at closing is $9.40 per square foot on a NNN basis, with 2.5 percent annual escalations. The going-in cap rate was 5.3 percent, implying in-place net operating income of approximately $12.2 million. The transaction was financed with a $148 million ten-year fixed-rate loan from Cornwater Life Insurance at 5.48 percent on a 30-year amortization schedule. Cascade's equity contributed $83 million through its third closed-end fund, Cascade Industrial Fund III. The Vernondale Eastbrook submarket recorded 6.4 million square feet of net absorption in 2025, with vacancy remaining below 3 percent. Meridian Industrial Advisors and Westport Capital Markets jointly brokered the transaction.

paragraph: Halcyon Residential Capital completed a $415 million preferred equity recapitalization of the Greenvale Towers Portfolio, a collection of three luxury high-rise apartment buildings totaling 1,104 units in the Greenvale neighborhood of Caldport. The preferred equity was placed with Monarch Sovereign Fund, a Korean pension-linked vehicle, at an 8-percent preferred return with a five-year term. The portfolio, developed and owned by Greenvale Residential Partners since 2019, was appraised at $695 million, reflecting average per-unit values of approximately $630,000. All three towers are stabilized, with a portfolio-wide occupancy rate of 96.7 percent and average in-place rents of $3,480 per month per unit. Annual net operating income is approximately $32.4 million, implying an overall going-in cap rate of 4.66 percent on the total appraised value. The recapitalization proceeds were used to retire $340 million in existing construction and bridge debt, with the residual equity distributed to the sponsor. Greenvale Residential Partners retains a promoted interest in the venture and continues as operating partner. Northgate Real Estate Capital structured the preferred equity transaction and advised Greenvale Residential Partners throughout the process.

paragraph: Trellis Realty Group executed the $53.8 million sale-leaseback of the Dunmore Corporate Center, a 198,000-square-foot single-tenant suburban office building located at 11200 Commerce Boulevard in the Dunmore submarket of Fairvale. The seller-lessee, Alderton Manufacturing Holdings, will continue to occupy the entirety of the building under a 15-year absolute triple-net lease at an initial rent of $16.50 per square foot, with 2-percent annual bumps. The going-in cap rate for the buyer, Kestrel Net Lease Income Fund, was 6.05 percent. Alderton Manufacturing Holdings intends to redeploy the $53.8 million in proceeds toward capital expenditures at two production facilities. The building, constructed in 2008, was most recently appraised at $56 million, suggesting a modest discount to appraised value. The facility features 24,000 square feet of executive office space, a 500-seat cafeteria, a 15,000-square-foot training center, and 820 surface parking spaces. Kestrel Net Lease Income Fund financed the acquisition with a $33 million ten-year fixed-rate mortgage from Greenfield Savings Association at a rate of 5.55 percent. Redfern Corporate Solutions represented Alderton Manufacturing Holdings in the transaction, and Vantage Net Lease Advisors acted for Kestrel.

paragraph: Ridgemont Office Partners and its institutional equity partner, Torchlight Capital Partners, announced the ground-up development of the Parkway Centre, a 380,000-square-foot Class A office building at 500 Innovation Parkway in the Northgate Business District of Cedarholm. Total project costs are estimated at $248 million, equating to $652 per square foot, with a targeted stabilized yield on cost of 7.2 percent and an estimated stabilized net operating income of $17.8 million. The project is being financed with a $164 million construction loan from a consortium of three regional banks, priced at SOFR plus 195 basis points over a 30-month term. Ridgemont and Torchlight have secured pre-lease commitments totaling 142,000 square feet, or 37 percent of the building, from two professional services firms: Kellner Advisory Group and Westbridge Litigation Support. Office asking rents in the Northgate Business District range from $38 to $46 per square foot on a full-service basis, among the highest in the Cedarholm metropolitan area. Groundbreaking is scheduled for July 2026, with shell completion targeted in the first quarter of 2028. Corning Realty Services is handling leasing assignments, with Thornwall Architecture and Eastbrook Builders serving as design and construction partners.

paragraph: Brightwater REIT, a publicly traded healthcare and medical office real estate investment trust, completed the $164.5 million acquisition of the Harmon Medical Pavilion, a 322,000-square-foot, four-building medical office campus adjacent to Harmon Regional Hospital in the Westport submarket of Alderton. The seller, Sunridge Health Properties, divested the campus as part of a strategic portfolio pruning initiative. Brightwater REIT is acquiring the asset at a 5.8 percent going-in cap rate, based on in-place net operating income of $9.54 million. The campus is 97.2 percent leased to seven physician groups, a regional hospital system, and two specialty outpatient surgery operators, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 8.3 years. Brightwater financed the acquisition with a $98 million ten-year fixed-rate mortgage from Pelham National Life at 5.65 percent and $66.5 million in equity sourced from its operating line. Post-closing, Brightwater REIT's total portfolio comprises 118 properties totaling 9.6 million square feet, with an aggregate gross asset value exceeding $2.8 billion. Halcrest Realty Advisors represented the seller. The transaction closed on April 9, 2026.

paragraph: Cormorant Capital Advisors, on behalf of its Opportunity Zone Fund II, executed the acquisition of the former Westlake Printing Works site, a 4.2-acre urban industrial property at 880 Westlake Avenue in the Ironside neighborhood of Halborn, for $21.4 million. The transaction enables Cormorant to develop a 310-unit mixed-income residential tower on the brownfield parcel, with 20 percent of units reserved for households earning below 80 percent of area median income, a condition required for Opportunity Zone basis step-up benefits. Total development cost is projected at $147 million, inclusive of site remediation estimated at $6.8 million. The project will be financed with a $98 million construction loan from Duncastle Community Bank and $49 million in Opportunity Zone equity from 16 individual and institutional investors. Cormorant anticipates breaking ground in December 2026 and delivering the first residential units in the second half of 2028. The 42-story tower will offer studio through three-bedroom units ranging from 485 to 1,240 square feet, with market-rate rents estimated at $2,100 to $3,600 per month. Ironside's residential vacancy rate stood at 2.8 percent as of March 2026, among the tightest in the Halborn metropolitan area.

paragraph: Northfield Realty Capital disclosed that its fourth non-traded REIT, Northfield Income Properties IV, completed the acquisition of Rivergate Commerce Center, a 1.05-million-square-foot multi-tenant industrial park in the South River submarket of Fordham, for $118 million, or $112.38 per square foot. The 11-building park was sold by Meridian Industrial Holdings following a seven-year hold during which Meridian invested $12.4 million in roof replacements, LED lighting retrofits, and dock-leveler upgrades. The park was 93.5 percent occupied at sale to 23 tenants, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 3.8 years and an average in-place rent of $8.40 per square foot, estimated to be 12 percent below market. The going-in cap rate was 5.95 percent, based on in-place net operating income of $7.03 million. Northfield Income Properties IV financed the acquisition with a $74 million floating-rate loan from Greyfield Commercial Mortgage at SOFR plus 205 basis points. Northfield intends to mark-to-market leases upon rollover, targeting a stabilized rent of $9.45 per square foot and net operating income of approximately $8.4 million at full stabilization. Westmont Industrial Brokerage and Velden Realty Group jointly brokered the sale.

paragraph: Langfield Self-Storage Partners announced the $88.6 million acquisition of a six-property self-storage portfolio comprising 4,340 units and approximately 512,000 net rentable square feet in the greater Millbrook metropolitan area. The seller, Fenwick Storage Group, assembled the portfolio between 2012 and 2020, with three facilities in suburban Millbrook, two in the adjacent community of Cresthaven, and one in Elmore Township. Portfolio-wide occupancy at closing was 91.3 percent, with an average effective street rate of $146 per unit per month. The going-in cap rate was 5.4 percent, based on in-place net operating income of $4.78 million. Langfield financed the acquisition with $57 million in financing provided by Portside Mortgage Corporation at a fixed rate of 5.62 percent over ten years with a 30-year amortization. The acquisition is consistent with Langfield's strategy of acquiring mid-size, suburban self-storage portfolios in markets with below-average new supply pipelines. The six properties are concentrated along the Route 74 corridor, which Langfield views as a supply-constrained submarket benefiting from strong household formation trends. Millbrook's self-storage vacancy rate stood at 6.8 percent as of year-end 2025. Clearwater Storage Advisors facilitated the transaction.

paragraph: Fenbrook Real Estate Fund VI, a value-add commingled fund managed by Fenbrook Capital Partners, closed a $92.4 million acquisition of the Copperfield Retail Center, a 425,000-square-foot power center located at the intersection of Copperfield Boulevard and Highway 62 in Sandton. The center is shadow-anchored by a national electronics retailer in a separately owned pad, with in-line tenants including a home improvement chain, a furniture retailer, two sporting goods chains, and multiple junior anchor tenants. Occupancy at acquisition was 81 percent, with 80,500 square feet of vacant space across four anchor bays. The going-in cap rate was 7.8 percent on occupied income. Fenbrook's business plan involves backfilling vacant boxes with medical office users and entertainment tenants, categories showing the strongest demand in the Sandton retail market. Fenbrook financed the transaction with a $58 million bridge loan from Redfern Bridge Capital at SOFR plus 305 basis points and $34.4 million in fund equity. The seller, Copperfield Partners LLC, a family-controlled operator, had held the asset since 2006. Targeted unlevered internal rate of return for the investment is 11.5 to 13 percent over a five-year hold. Eastgate Realty Advisors brokered the sale.

paragraph: Aldwick Realty Capital Trust, a publicly traded diversified REIT, completed a $500 million unsecured notes offering, with $250 million of 5.45 percent notes due 2031 and $250 million of 5.90 percent notes due 2036. Net proceeds will be used to repay $375 million in floating-rate secured debt and to fund near-term property acquisitions. Following the offering, Aldwick Realty Capital Trust's weighted average interest rate declines from 5.61 percent to 5.49 percent, and its weighted average debt maturity extends from 4.2 years to 5.8 years. The company's total capitalization is approximately $9.4 billion, with a debt-to-total-enterprise-value ratio of 38 percent. Aldwick's portfolio comprises 224 properties totaling 28.7 million square feet, spanning industrial, office, and retail asset classes. The offering was led by Greyfield Investment Bank, Calderton Securities, and Vantage Capital Markets. The notes are rated investment grade at BBB/Baa2 by the two applicable rating agencies. paragraph: Harwick Real Estate Partners completed the sale of the Harwick Commerce District, a 78-acre master-planned industrial park in the Harwick Township submarket of Trentville, in a portfolio disposition valued at $343 million. paragraph: Duncastle Property Trust, a publicly traded office and mixed-use REIT, announced the recapitalization of the Duncastle Innovation Quarter, a 2.3-million-square-foot urban campus in the Midway neighborhood of Clearwater that was originally developed by the trust between 2010 and 2019. paragraph: Meridian Equity Partners and co-investor Westbrook Institutional Fund completed the $178 million acquisition of the Fairlawn Promenade, a 495,000-square-foot open-air lifestyle center in the affluent Fairlawn submarket of Greenvale. Meridian and Westbrook plan a $14.5 million refresh of the center's common areas, landscaping, signage, and seasonal programming infrastructure over the first 24 months of ownership. Eastgate Retail Advisors exclusively represented the seller.

paragraph: Thornbury Industrial Fund II, a closed-end value-add fund managed by Thornbury Capital Group, secured a $41 million senior mortgage refinancing of the Elmwood Distribution Hub, a 298,000-square-foot multi-tenant industrial park in the Elmwood submarket of Portcliffe. Whitmore Commercial Mortgage arranged the refinancing.

paragraph: Elmore Realty Trust, a publicly traded net-lease REIT focused on essential retail, announced the completion of its disposition of 35 properties for total proceeds of $187.3 million, reflecting a blended exit cap rate of 5.9 percent. Redfern Net Lease Advisors ran the marketing process for all three portfolio transactions.

paragraph: Crestview Residential Partners announced the $67.3 million preferred equity investment by Argent Institutional Partners in the Crestview Summit, a 482-unit luxury multifamily high-rise under construction at 2400 Summit Drive in the Ridgecrest neighborhood of Portsmith. The project includes ground-floor retail totaling 4,800 square feet and below-grade parking for 420 vehicles. Portsmith's Ridgecrest submarket absorbed 1,640 new apartment units in 2025, with market occupancy remaining at 95.1 percent. Pelham Structured Capital advised Crestview on the preferred equity placement.

paragraph: Dunmore Capital Trust announced the $249 million refinancing of the Dunmore Tower, a 45-story, 960,000-square-foot Class A office skyscraper in the Central Business District of Calderton. The new financing, originated by a three-lender syndicate comprising Aldwick National Bank, Riverton Life Insurance, and Greyfield Mortgage Trust, replaces $198 million in existing CMBS debt that was approaching its final maturity date. The tower is 91 percent leased to 18 tenants, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 6.2 years and effective gross annual income of approximately $48 million. Since acquiring the tower in 2014 for $312 million, Dunmore Capital Trust has invested $38 million in capital improvements including elevator modernization, lobby renovation, and the addition of a tenant conference center and fitness facility. Westbridge Commercial Finance arranged the refinancing.

paragraph: Lakemont Property Partners completed the sale of the Lakemont Corporate Center, a 16-acre, five-building suburban office campus totaling 437,000 square feet in the Lakemont Business Park submarket of Grenville, for $38.5 million, or $88.10 per square foot. paragraph: Falconer Development Partners broke ground on the Westgate Commerce Center, a three-phase, 4.5-million-square-foot speculative industrial development on a 295-acre greenfield site in the Westgate Township submarket of Veldran. Falconer anticipates initiating lease negotiations as soon as framing is complete, targeting a combination of single-tenant buildouts and multi-tenant configurations for Phase I. Phases II and III are contingent on Phase I lease-up performance, with Phase II planned for 1.6 million square feet and Phase III for 1.7 million square feet. Cornhill Industrial Leasing is handling all Phase I marketing assignments.

paragraph: Eastbrook REIT, a publicly traded diversified real estate investment trust, executed a 1031 exchange transaction, disposing of the Eastbrook Office Plaza, a 215,000-square-foot suburban office building in Alderton, for $29.8 million, and simultaneously acquiring the Thornfield Logistics Hub, a newly constructed 680,000-square-foot industrial facility in the Thornfield submarket of Veldran, for $81.4 million. paragraph: Aldgate Real Estate Securities, a commercial real estate structured finance vehicle, closed a $340 million CMBS conduit transaction backed by 18 commercial real estate loans originated across eight states. The pool includes eight retail properties, six industrial facilities, two multifamily communities, one hotel, and one mixed-use asset. The aggregate outstanding principal balance of the collateral pool is $340 million, with a weighted average loan-to-value ratio of 61.3 percent, a weighted average debt service coverage ratio of 1.72x, and a weighted average going-in cap rate of 5.85 percent. The transaction was structured in six tranches, with the senior AAA-rated class representing $231 million priced at a spread of 72 basis points over the comparable-maturity swap rate. Subordinate tranches were placed with a mix of B-piece buyers, insurance accounts, and CMBS-focused hedge funds. The pool's weighted average interest rate is 5.96 percent, and the weighted average remaining loan term is 8.7 years. Corning Investment Bank and Westbrook Capital Markets co-managed the securitization. Aldgate Real Estate Securities plans to issue a subsequent CMBS conduit in the third quarter of 2026, targeting a pool balance of $420 million. Rating agencies Westmark Ratings and Cordate Analytics assigned ratings to all six tranches.

paragraph: Thornwall Realty Trust, a publicly traded healthcare-focused REIT, entered into a definitive agreement to acquire a seven-property senior housing portfolio comprising 892 independent living, assisted living, and memory care units in the southeastern United States, for total consideration of $218 million, or $244,395 per unit.
